component_identification.content:
#### Cards
- 183 dragon cards total: 148 full-grown dragons + 35 hatchlings (a new card type requiring sustained investment to grow)
- 75 cave cards — must be played to excavate spaces before dragons can be placed
- 10 objective tiles (double-sided = 20 possible objectives), 4 used per game
- Quick-start cards for learning the game

#### Player Components (per player)
- 1 player mat with 3 cavern rows (Crimson Cavern, Golden Grotto, Amethyst Abyss), each with 4 dragon slots
- 1 adventurer meeple (moves through caverns during explore actions)
- 1 guild token (tracks position on the Dragon Guild)
- 8 player markers (for claiming Dragon Guild tile benefits)

#### Resources & Tokens
- 100 resource tokens in 4 types: Meat (red), Gold (yellow), Crystals (purple), Milk (white)
- 55 egg tokens (speckled) — worth 1 VP each at game end AND used as escalation costs
- 45 coin tokens — coins are NOT resources; they represent actions (1 coin = 1 action)
- 20 multiplier tokens

#### Shared Components
- 1 Dragon Guild board with circular track
- 4 Dragon Guild tiles (double-sided: one side for 2-3 players, other for 4-5 players)
- 1 card display board (holds face-up dragon cards for drafting)
- 1 round tracker board
- Round marker and start player marker
- 17 Automa/solo cards
- 2 rulebooks (multiplayer + solo) and 1 dragon fact book

core_game_loop.content:
#### Game Structure
Wyrmspan plays over exactly **4 rounds**. Each round, every player receives **6 coins**. Each action costs 1 coin, so you get at least 6 actions per round — but you can earn bonus coins during play, potentially taking more. Unspent coins at round end are wasted.

#### Three Action Types (Each Costs 1 Coin)

**1. Excavate** — Play a cave card from your hand into the leftmost empty slot of any cavern.
- Column 2 costs 1 coin only. Column 3 costs 1 coin + 1 egg. Column 4 costs 1 coin + 2 eggs.
- The cave card's "when played" bonus activates immediately (resources, cards, eggs, guild advancement).
- You MUST excavate a space before you can place a dragon there (column 1 of each cave is pre-excavated).

**2. Entice a Dragon** — Play a dragon card from your hand into an excavated slot.
- Pay the dragon's resource cost (meat, gold, crystals, milk, and/or eggs).
- Place in the leftmost excavated-but-empty slot of the matching cavern.
- Resolve the dragon's "when played" ability if it has one.

**3. Explore a Cavern** — Send your adventurer through a cavern to activate dragons.
- Choose a cavern. Your adventurer moves left to right, activating each dragon's ability in sequence.
- At the end of the row, gain the explore reward printed on your player mat (resources, cards, cave cards, or guild advancement depending on the cavern).
- Exploring the same cavern multiple times per round costs escalating eggs: free first time, 1 egg second time, 2 eggs third time.

#### Engine Building
The key mechanic: exploring a cavern with 4 dragons triggers all 4 abilities in sequence, then the mat reward. A full Crimson Cavern exploration might generate 4+ resources in one action. This is how engines compound — identical to Wingspan's habitat activation, but left-to-right instead of right-to-left.

#### Between Rounds
Score the round objective. Gain 6 new coins + 1 egg. Begin next round.

detailed_rules.content:
#### Excavation Details
- Cave cards are separate from dragon cards — they're the "infrastructure" layer
- Each cave card has a "when played" bonus: resources, eggs, guild advancement, or special abilities
- Column 1 of each cavern is pre-excavated at game start (no cave card needed)
- Columns 2-4 must be excavated with cave cards before dragons can be placed
- Cave cards in hand at game end are worth nothing (but convert at 4 items = 1 VP)

#### Dragon Placement Rules
- Dragons must go in their allowed cavern (shown on the card)
- Must be placed in the leftmost excavated-but-empty slot
- Pay the resource cost shown on the card
- Some dragons can go in multiple caverns (multiple cave icons shown)

#### Dragon Types
- **Full-grown dragons (148)**: Standard cards with abilities and VP values
- **Hatchlings (35)**: Require sustained investment — you cache resources (meat/milk) or tuck cards onto them over multiple turns. Once they reach their threshold (usually 3 cached items), their powerful third-line ability triggers once. High investment, high payoff.

#### Dragon Ability Types
- **When Played** — One-time trigger on placement. Immediate bonus.
- **When Activated** — Triggers every time you explore this cavern. The engine-building core.
- **Once Per Round** — Triggers once each round. Consistent but not chainable.
- **End of Game** — Bonus VP at final scoring based on board state.

#### Dragon Personalities
Dragons have personality tags: aggressive, helpful, playful, or shy. Some dragons grant bonuses for adjacent personality matches or specific personality counts in a cavern. Consider this when choosing placement.

#### The Three Caverns

**Crimson Cavern (Red / Top)**
- Preferred resource: Meat
- Explore reward: Resources from supply + Dragon Guild advancement
- Role: Primary resource-generation engine

**Golden Grotto (Yellow / Middle)**
- Preferred resource: Gold
- Explore reward: Dragon cards from display + Guild advancement
- Role: Card-drawing and tucking engine (tucked cards = 1 VP each)

**Amethyst Abyss (Purple / Bottom)**
- Preferred resource: Crystals
- Explore reward: Cave cards from supply + Guild advancement
- Role: Cave card engine and hatchling nursery

#### Dragon Guild Track
- Circular track on a shared board. Advance by exploring (printed icons), playing certain cards, or abilities.
- Landing on spaces grants bonuses: resources, coins, cards, eggs.
- When advancing multiple spaces, gain ALL bonuses passed through.
- **Brown spaces**: Must place a player marker on the Guild tile and gain its benefit. Max 4 markers per player.
- Guild tile has 5 abilities; 4th and 5th slots (end-game scoring) are highest priority.
- Two-sided tile: one side for 2-3 players, other for 4-5 players.

#### Resource Rules
- 4 resource types: Meat, Gold, Crystals, Milk
- **Coins are NOT resources** — they represent actions. Abilities that discount resources don't affect coin costs.
- **2-for-1 conversion**: At any time during your turn, exchange 2 resources (any types) for 1 of another type. Emergency valve.
- **9-item hand limit**: At end of turn, you cannot exceed 9 total items (cards + resources). Discard excess.
- **Eggs**: Dual purpose — 1 VP each at game end AND used as escalation costs for exploring/excavating.

#### Column 4 Special Ability
The 4th column slot has a "when covered" ability: spend 3 items (any combination of resources, dragon cards, cave cards) to gain 1 coin. Since coins = actions, this is extremely valuable.

#### End-Game Conversion
At game end, every 4 leftover items (cards in hand + unspent resources) convert to 1 VP.

#### Setup
1. Each player takes a player mat, adventurer meeple, guild token, and 8 markers
2. Column 1 of each cavern is pre-excavated
3. Shuffle dragon deck, reveal face-up display. Shuffle cave card deck.
4. Set up Dragon Guild board and randomly select Guild tile side
5. Place 4 random objective tiles (one per round)
6. Deal each player 5 dragon cards, 3 cave cards, and 2 objective cards
7. Keep 3 dragons, discard 2. Keep 1 objective, discard 1.
8. Choose 3 starting resources (any combination of meat, gold, crystals, milk)
9. Each player starts with 0 eggs and 6 coins for round 1

scoring_and_endgame.content:
#### Game End
The game ends after Round 4.

#### Victory Point Sources
1. **Dragon VP** — Printed value on each dragon in your caverns
2. **Eggs** — 1 VP per egg on your dragons or storage at game end
3. **Tucked cards** — 1 VP per card tucked under dragons
4. **Cached resources** — 1 VP per resource cached on dragons (for hatchlings)
5. **Round objectives** — VP from 4 round objectives (later rounds worth more)
6. **Dragon Guild tile** — VP from claimed Guild tile abilities (especially 4th/5th slots)
7. **End-game dragon abilities** — Some dragons score bonus VP based on board state
8. **Leftover conversion** — Every 4 leftover items (cards + resources) = 1 VP

#### Typical Winning Scores
- 2-player: 85-110 VP
- 3-player: 80-100 VP
- 4-5 player: 75-95 VP
- Experienced play: 100-130+ VP

#### Tiebreaker
Most remaining resources wins ties.

beginner_strategy.content:
#### Key Principles

- **Coins = actions. Never waste them.** You get 6 coins per round. Unspent coins at round end are gone forever. Spend them all.

- **Excavate first, then entice, then explore.** Don't explore empty caverns — your adventurer hits a dead end immediately. The optimal early rhythm: Excavate → Entice dragon → Excavate → Entice dragon → Then start exploring.

- **Focus one cavern.** A full cavern (4 dragons) explored generates 4 ability activations. Two dragons in each of 3 caves generates far less. Focus beats breadth.

- **Crimson Cavern first.** Getting resource-generating dragons into Crimson early means every exploration there fuels the rest of your board.

- **The Dragon Guild matters.** It's not optional flavor — it's a core resource and scoring engine. Advance whenever possible, especially to claim end-game scoring Guild tile slots.

- **Eggs are precious.** They score 1 VP each AND are used for escalation costs. Save them for exploring (which generates more value) over excavation when possible.

- **Don't hoard.** 9-item hand limit. Cards and resources in hand score almost nothing. Play them.

#### Common Beginner Mistakes
- Exploring before placing dragons (empty caves = wasted actions)
- Letting coins go unspent at round end (wasted actions!)
- Ignoring the Dragon Guild (one of the biggest VP sources)
- Spending eggs on excavation when Guild abilities or cave cards could excavate for free
- Spreading evenly across 3 caverns instead of focusing one
- Not reading all 4 round objectives at setup to plan your game arc
- Playing expensive high-VP dragons too early instead of cheap engine pieces

walkthrough.content:
#### Your First Game of Wyrmspan — A Guided Walkthrough

**If You've Played Wingspan**
Wyrmspan is ~85% the same core design, reimplemented with dragons and several key refinements:
- **No dice.** Resources come from exploring caverns and the Guild track — predictable, not random.
- **Coins = actions.** 6 coins per round (not Wingspan's shrinking 8/7/6/5). You can earn bonus coins.
- **Two-step placement.** You must excavate a space (play a cave card) before placing a dragon. Two actions instead of one.
- **Dragon Guild.** A new circular track with cascading bonuses — prevents the Wingspan problem of wasting turns on minor resource needs.
- **Hatchlings.** 35 dragons that require multi-turn investment but pay off big.
- **Explore left-to-right** (Wingspan activates right-to-left).

**Setup**
You get a player mat with 3 cavern rows (4 slots each, column 1 pre-excavated). Keep 3 of 5 dragon cards, 1 of 2 objectives. Choose 3 starting resources. You start with 6 coins and 0 eggs.

**Round 1 (6 coins = 6+ actions)**

*Action 1*: **Excavate** column 2 of your Crimson Cavern. Play a cave card from your hand — gain its "when played" bonus (maybe resources or a guild advance). Cost: 1 coin.

*Action 2*: **Entice a dragon** into Crimson Cavern column 1 (pre-excavated). Pick a dragon with a strong "when activated" ability. Pay its resource cost. Cost: 1 coin.

*Action 3*: **Entice a dragon** into the slot you just excavated (Crimson column 2). Now you have 2 dragons ready to activate! Cost: 1 coin.

*Action 4*: **Explore Crimson Cavern.** Your adventurer moves left-to-right, activating both dragons' abilities (resources, cards, etc.), then gains the mat's explore reward. Cost: 1 coin.

*Actions 5-6*: Excavate another cavern, entice more dragons, or explore again (2nd explore costs 1 egg). Spend all your coins!

**Rounds 2-3: Engine Building**
Keep filling your focus cavern. A 4-dragon Crimson Cavern explored = 4 ability activations + mat reward. That's potentially 4+ resources per action.

Start advancing on the Dragon Guild track — race to claim the end-game scoring slots (4th and 5th abilities) before opponents.

Watch round objectives — they escalate in VP. Don't chase all 4, but score the ones that align with your engine.

**Round 4: Scoring Push**
Entice high-VP dragons. Lay eggs (1 VP each). Complete any hatchlings that are close to their threshold. Check your objective.

**Scoring**
Dragon VP + eggs + tucked cards + cached resources + objectives + Guild tile + end-game abilities + leftover conversion (4 items = 1 VP).

**Target**: 70-85 VP first game. Focus on filling one cavern deep.

advanced_strategy.content:
#### Wingspan vs Wyrmspan — Key Strategic Differences

- **No dice randomness.** Resources are predictable from exploration. Plan precisely.
- **Coins = actions.** Earning bonus coins (from column 4 ability, Guild, or dragon powers) effectively gives you extra turns. This is the biggest strategic lever in Wyrmspan.
- **Two-step placement** (excavate + entice) means board development is slower. Engine pieces need to come online by Round 2 to compound.
- **Guild track is a major VP and resource engine.** There's nothing like it in Wingspan. Players who advance quickly gain cascading bonuses.
- **Hatchlings reward sustained investment.** No Wingspan equivalent. They're like mini-quests that pay off when you cache enough items.

#### Core Engine Types

**Resource Engine (Crimson Focus — Safest)**
- Fill Crimson with dragons that generate meat/gold/crystals on activation.
- Each exploration generates cascading resources to fuel enticing elsewhere.
- Best all-around strategy. Provides the resource base for everything else.

**Card/Tucking Engine (Golden Focus)**
- Fill Golden Grotto with dragons that draw or tuck cards.
- Tucked cards = 1 VP each (plus any bonus from the dragon's end-game ability).
- Generates card flow to keep options open. Good for combo-hunting.

**Cave Card Engine (Amethyst Focus — Hatchling Path)**
- Fill Amethyst to generate cave cards through exploration.
- Self-sustaining excavation pipeline. Best for hatchling strategies since most hatchlings go here.
- Higher variance but potentially highest ceiling.

**Recommended**: Go deep in 1 cavern by Round 3 (4 dragons), light presence in a 2nd.

#### Dragon Guild Optimization

**Guild Tile Priority (when placing markers):**
1. 4th ability (end-game scoring) — Claim first. Compounds.
2. 5th ability (end-game scoring) — Second priority.
3. 3rd ability (extra action/coin with bonuses) — If scoring slots taken.
4. 1st/2nd abilities (discounted excavation/enticement) — Fallback.

**Racing matters.** At 4-5 players, Guild tile spaces are contested. Sprint to brown spaces early by choosing dragons with guild advancement on their activation abilities.

#### Hatchling Strategy

- Place hatchlings early (Round 1-2), especially in Amethyst Abyss.
- Each exploration lets you cache a resource or tuck a card onto the hatchling.
- Once threshold hit (usually 3 cached items), the powerful third-line ability fires once.
- If you commit to 1-2 hatchlings early, the payoff is significant.
- If you can't commit to feeding them, don't draft them.

#### Coin Optimization

- Every unspent coin at round end is a wasted action. **Spend all 6 coins minimum.**
- Column 4's "spend 3 items for 1 coin" ability is one of the best effects in the game — an extra action.
- Some Guild track spaces give coins. Some dragons give coins on activation.
- A player who averages 7-8 actions per round (from bonus coins) crushes someone who takes only 6.

#### Round Objective Strategy

- Study all 4 objectives at setup. Let them inform starting card/resource choices.
- Later objectives are worth more VP — it's correct to sacrifice Round 1-2 objectives for Round 3-4 dominance.
- Having zero of what an objective measures = automatic 0 VP regardless of others' scores. Avoid zeros.
- Common types: dragons in a specific cavern, eggs, cached resources, tucked cards, personality counts, guild position.

#### Player Count Adjustments

**2-3 Players:**
- Guild tile is less contested — be deliberate about marker placement.
- Card display refreshes slowly — you can plan around visible dragons.
- Round objectives less competitive.

**4-5 Players:**
- Guild tile fiercely contested — race for 4th/5th slots.
- Card display churns fast — don't plan around specific face-up dragons.
- Round objectives more unpredictable — watch opponents' boards.

#### Key Decisions

1. **Setup: Starting resources.** Choose 3 resources that support your starting dragon cards AND your first Guild tile advancement.
2. **Round 1: Excavate-first tempo.** Don't explore until you have 2+ dragons in a cavern. Excavate → Entice → Excavate → Entice → Then explore.
3. **Round 2: Focus cavern.** Commit to filling one cavern to 4 dragons for maximum exploration chains.
4. **Round 3: Guild tile race.** Reach brown spaces to claim end-game scoring before opponents.
5. **Round 4: Scoring shift.** Stop building engine, start scoring. High-VP dragons, eggs, objective completion.

metadata.notes: Deep dive content sprint — DTW prep, March 2026. Research-corrected: coin-based actions, cave card excavation, hatchlings, dragon personalities, 4 slots per cave, no dice. Sources: official rules, BGG strategy primer, community guides.

subtopics.title: Components
subtopics.content:
#### Cards
- **183 Dragon Cards**: 148 full-grown dragons + 35 hatchlings (require sustained investment to grow)
- **75 Cave Cards** — played to excavate spaces before dragons can be placed
- **10 Objective Tiles** (double-sided = 20 possible), 4 used per game

#### Player Components
- **Player Mat** with 3 cavern rows (4 slots each): Crimson Cavern, Golden Grotto, Amethyst Abyss
- **Adventurer Meeple** — moves through caverns during explore actions
- **Guild Token** — tracks Dragon Guild position
- **8 Player Markers** — for claiming Guild tile benefits

#### Resources & Tokens
- **Meat** (red), **Gold** (yellow), **Crystals** (purple), **Milk** (white) — 100 total
- **55 Eggs** — 1 VP each at end + used as escalation costs
- **45 Coins** — NOT resources; 1 coin = 1 action
- **20 Multiplier tokens**

#### Shared
- Dragon Guild board + 4 double-sided Guild tiles (2-3p side / 4-5p side)
- Card display board, round tracker
- Solo mode: 17 Automa cards

subtopics.title: Setup
subtopics.content:
#### Setup Steps
1. Each player takes player mat (3 caves, column 1 pre-excavated), adventurer, guild token, 8 markers
2. Shuffle dragon deck, set up face-up display. Shuffle cave card deck.
3. Select Dragon Guild tile side (2-3p or 4-5p)
4. Place 4 random objective tiles
5. Deal each player: 5 dragon cards + 3 cave cards + 2 objectives
6. Keep 3 dragons, discard 2. Keep 1 objective, discard 1.
7. Choose 3 starting resources (any combo of meat/gold/crystals/milk)
8. Each player starts with 0 eggs, 6 coins for Round 1
9. Determine first player

subtopics.title: Actions & Turn Structure
subtopics.content:
#### Round Structure
4 rounds. Each round: receive 6 coins + 1 egg. Take turns spending coins on actions.

#### Three Actions (each costs 1 coin):

**1. Excavate** — Play a cave card into leftmost empty slot of any cavern.
- Column 2: 1 coin. Column 3: 1 coin + 1 egg. Column 4: 1 coin + 2 eggs.
- Cave card's "when played" bonus activates immediately.
- MUST excavate before placing dragons (column 1 is pre-excavated).

**2. Entice Dragon** — Play a dragon card into an excavated slot.
- Pay resource cost. Place in leftmost excavated-but-empty slot.
- Resolve "when played" ability if any.

**3. Explore Cavern** — Adventurer moves left-to-right activating each dragon.
- At end, gain mat's explore reward (varies by cavern).
- Exploring same cavern again costs eggs: free → 1 egg → 2 eggs.

#### Coins
- 6 coins per round minimum. Bonus coins possible from Guild, abilities, column 4.
- Unspent coins at round end are WASTED. Spend them all.

#### Hand Limit
9 items maximum (cards + resources). Discard excess at end of turn.

subtopics.title: The Three Caverns
subtopics.content:
#### Crimson Cavern (Red / Top)
- Preferred resource: Meat
- Explore reward: Resources + Dragon Guild advancement
- Role: Primary resource engine. Get dragons here first.

#### Golden Grotto (Yellow / Middle)
- Preferred resource: Gold
- Explore reward: Dragon cards + Guild advancement
- Role: Card flow and tucking engine (tucked cards = 1 VP each)

#### Amethyst Abyss (Purple / Bottom)
- Preferred resource: Crystals
- Explore reward: Cave cards + Guild advancement
- Role: Cave card pipeline + hatchling nursery

#### Exploring Mechanics
- Adventurer moves left to right through the cavern
- Each dragon's ability activates in sequence
- At end, gain the mat's printed explore reward
- Full cavern (4 dragons) = 4 activations + reward = massive value per action

subtopics.title: Dragon Guild & Abilities
subtopics.content:
#### Dragon Guild Track
- Circular shared track. Advance by exploring (printed icons), card abilities, etc.
- Landing = gain that space's bonus. Passing through = gain ALL bonuses along the way.
- **Brown spaces**: Place a player marker on Guild tile, gain its benefit. Max 4 markers.
- Guild tile priority: 4th slot (end-game VP) > 5th slot > 3rd slot > 1st/2nd.

#### Dragon Ability Types
- **When Played**: One-time immediate bonus
- **When Activated**: Triggers every exploration (engine core!)
- **Once Per Round**: Consistent per-round trigger
- **End of Game**: VP at final scoring

#### Hatchlings
- 35 of 183 dragons. Require caching resources/tucking cards over multiple turns.
- When threshold reached (usually 3 items), powerful third-line ability fires once.
- High investment, high payoff. Place early if committing.

#### Dragon Personalities
Aggressive, helpful, playful, shy. Some dragons score for adjacent personality matches.

#### Key Rules
- Coins are NOT resources (ability discounts don't apply)
- 2-for-1 resource conversion anytime during your turn
- End-game: every 4 leftover items = 1 VP

subtopics.title: Scoring & End Game
subtopics.content:
#### Game End
After Round 4.

#### VP Sources
1. Dragon VP (printed values)
2. Eggs (1 VP each)
3. Tucked cards (1 VP each)
4. Cached resources (1 VP each on hatchlings)
5. Round objectives (4 total, later rounds worth more)
6. Dragon Guild tile markers (especially end-game scoring slots)
7. End-game dragon abilities
8. Leftover conversion (4 items = 1 VP)

#### Tiebreaker
Most remaining resources.

subtopics.title: Beginner Strategy
subtopics.content:
#### Key Principles
- **Coins = actions. Spend them all.** 6 coins = 6 actions minimum. Unspent = wasted.
- **Excavate first, then entice, then explore.** Empty caves = wasted explorations.
- **Focus one cavern.** 4 dragons explored > 2 dragons in each of 3 caves.
- **Crimson first.** Resource-generating dragons fuel everything else.
- **Dragon Guild matters.** Core resource + scoring engine, not optional.
- **Eggs are precious.** 1 VP each + escalation costs. Save for exploring over excavation.

#### Common Mistakes
- Exploring before placing dragons
- Unspent coins at round end
- Ignoring Dragon Guild
- Spreading across all 3 caverns
- Not reading all 4 objectives at setup

subtopics.title: First-Game Walkthrough
subtopics.content:
#### If You've Played Wingspan
Wyrmspan is ~85% the same core, reimplemented with key refinements:
- **No dice.** Resources from exploration are predictable.
- **Coins = actions.** 6/round (not shrinking 8/7/6/5). Earn bonus coins.
- **Two-step placement.** Excavate (cave card) then entice (dragon). Two actions.
- **Dragon Guild.** Circular track with cascading bonuses. No Wingspan equivalent.
- **Hatchlings.** 35 dragons requiring multi-turn investment.
- **Left-to-right** exploration (Wingspan is right-to-left).

**Setup**: 3 caves, 4 slots each (column 1 pre-excavated). Keep 3 of 5 dragons, 1 of 2 objectives. Choose 3 resources. Start with 6 coins, 0 eggs.

**Round 1 (6 coins)**
*Action 1*: Excavate Crimson column 2 (play a cave card, gain bonus). 1 coin.
*Action 2*: Entice a dragon into Crimson column 1 (pre-excavated). 1 coin.
*Action 3*: Entice another dragon into Crimson column 2. 1 coin.
*Action 4*: Explore Crimson — 2 dragon activations + mat reward! 1 coin.
*Actions 5-6*: Excavate/entice in another cave, or explore again. Spend all coins!

**Rounds 2-3**: Fill focus cavern to 4 dragons. Advance Dragon Guild. Watch objectives.
**Round 4**: High-VP dragons, eggs, complete hatchlings, check objective.

**Target**: 70-85 VP. Focus one cavern deep.

subtopics.title: Advanced Strategy
subtopics.content:
#### Wingspan vs Wyrmspan Strategic Differences
- No dice = precise resource planning
- Bonus coins = extra actions. The #1 strategic lever.
- Two-step placement = slower development. Engine by Round 2 or fall behind.
- Guild track = major VP + resource source. No Wingspan equivalent.
- Hatchlings = mini-quests for sustained investment.

#### Engine Types
**Crimson Focus (Safest)**: Resource-generating dragons. Cascading resources per exploration.
**Golden Focus (Cards)**: Tucking engine (1 VP per tuck) + card flow for combos.
**Amethyst Focus (Hatchlings)**: Self-sustaining cave card pipeline. Highest ceiling, highest variance.
**Recommended**: Go deep in 1 cavern (4 dragons by Round 3), light 2nd.

#### Guild Tile Priority
1. 4th slot (end-game scoring) — claim first
2. 5th slot (end-game scoring)
3. 3rd slot (extra action/coin)
4. 1st/2nd slots (discounted actions)
At 4-5 players, race for brown spaces — Guild tile is contested.

#### Coin Optimization
- Spend all 6+ coins per round. Unspent = wasted turns.
- Column 4 ability (3 items → 1 coin) = extra action. Very powerful.
- Seek dragons/Guild bonuses that grant coins.
- 7-8 actions/round crushes 6.

#### Hatchling Strategy
- Place early (R1-2) in Amethyst. Feed via exploration.
- 3 cached items triggers powerful one-time ability.
- Commit early or don't draft them.

#### Round Objectives
- Study all 4 at setup. Later rounds worth more VP.
- Sacrifice R1-2 for R3-4 dominance if needed.
- Zero = automatic 0 VP. Avoid zeros.

#### Player Count
- **2-3P**: Guild less contested. Display refreshes slowly — plan around it.
- **4-5P**: Guild fiercely contested. Display churns. Watch opponents.

#### Key Decisions
1. Setup: 3 starting resources supporting your dragons + first Guild advance
2. R1: Excavate→Entice→Excavate→Entice→Explore rhythm
3. R2: Commit focus cavern (4 dragons)
4. R3: Race Guild tile for end-game scoring
5. R4: Stop building, start scoring
